engineering.translation: Gradually, by degrees (śanaiḥ śanaiḥ), one should arrest (uparamet) — buddhyā (by intellect) dhṛti-gṛhītayā (held by firmness); making the mind ātma-saṁstham (fixed on the Self), one should not think (na cintayet) anything at all (na kiñcid api). 6.25 specifies the gradualist procedure for the prior verse's restraint. The instrument is buddhi held firm by dhṛti; the result-state is the mind fixed on the Self alone. Shankara: with buddhi endowed with steady firmness, withdraw the mind by degrees; fix it on the Self; do not let it think anything else. Ramanuja (paired 6.24-25): relinquishing both classes of desires, restraining senses by mind, gradually become situated. The engineering analog is gradual-discipline — years of compounding practice — and is honest about not promising quick results. STRETCHED at full metaphysical scope.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A backend engineer two years into a deliberate practice of single-task focus opens her workday with one declared problem — today, the deadlock pattern in the order-fulfilment service. Her morning's procedure: open Sentry; pin the relevant traces; put the Slack laptop in another room; set Gmail to vacation-responder for the next three hours. When her mind drifts — a flash of curiosity about the Hacker News front page — she does not punish the drift; she returns. When the drift recurs at minute thirty-two — a flicker of anxiety about the upcoming review — she returns again. The procedure is not heroic. Across two years she has gradually (śanaiḥ śanaiḥ) increased the duration of single-task focus from forty minutes to two hours. Today she works for ninety minutes before the first significant drift. The buddhi held by dhṛti is the noticing-faculty trained over the months; the ātma-saṁstham state is the work as the mind's resting object. Compare the engineer who attempted to leap from forty-minute focus to four-hour focus in a week and abandoned the practice when it failed. The verse's gradualism is operational: the duration grows by degrees. Narrowed engineering rung; STRETCHED at full metaphysical reach.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader takes śanaiḥ śanaiḥ as licence for indefinite slow progress without check. The verse names gradual practice; it does not name absent practice. An engineer who reads 'gradually' as 'someday' has missed the gradient.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer's apparent gradualism is in fact perpetual deferral — always promising the focus practice will start next month — the verse does not endorse this. Uparamet is an active verb. The gradient is real, but a gradient with zero slope is not the verse.
engineering.implication: Pick an honest baseline of your current focus duration. The verse asks for gradual increase from that baseline, not a leap. The buddhi-held-by-dhṛti is the trained noticing-and-returning faculty itself.
engineering.quotable_line: Gradual restraint, by degrees, with intellect held firm — the duration grows, the leap is not the practice.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 6.25 names the gradualist procedure. Engineering rung at compounded-practice scope. Buddhi-dhṛti instrument and ātma-saṁstham state preserved. Shankara/Ramanuja preserved. Counter-example distinguishes gradualism from perpetual deferral. Tools: Sentry, Slack, Gmail, Hacker News. Confidence MEDIUM at narrow scope.
